summary: Iteration 4 backend testing complete. 50/50 pytest cases pass (all 26 prior tests + 24 new iteration-4 tests). Verified: Ethical Penalty default category seeding & shape, GET/PATCH /api/settings (with 422 rejection for invalid profile/frequency), behavioral multipliers (aggressive 1.5x, mindful 0.5x, ethical 1.4x on Fast Food/Clothes/Entertainment/Ethical Penalty only — Coffee/Transport untouched), Ethical Penalty category precedence under ethical profile (McDonalds -> Ethical Penalty cat, rate 0.49), Savings Beast auto_transfer triggered with total>=5 and all events transferred, pause_all_taxes returns {paused:true} and creates no tax_events, profile_applied persisted on every tax_event and surfaced in /api/activity, /api/insights/summary now exposes profile_type, Revolut OAuth scaffolding (consent_url contains client_id=05f4b015-..., response_type=code, scope=accounts, redirect_uri, state uuid; is_active=false; not listed in /bank/accounts), and Revolut legacy direct-token path still active=true.

minor.endpoint: POST /api/tax/process (Ethical Penalty precedence under non-ethical profiles)
minor.issue: _match_category sorts categories so 'Ethical Penalty' is checked FIRST regardless of user's profile_type. Side-effect: balanced/aggressive/mindful users whose merchants hit Ethical Penalty keywords (e.g. McDonalds, KFC, Primark, H&M, Amazon, Shein) will be categorised as 'Ethical Penalty' (base 0.35) instead of 'Fast Food' (0.30) / 'Clothes' (0.15) / 'Other' (0.10). The spec says Ethical Penalty multiplier is only for ethical profiles, but the routing itself (and thus the higher base rate 0.35) leaks to all profiles. If unintended, gate the precedence inside _match_category on profile_type=='ethical'. Reference: /app/backend/server.py:828-839 + /app/backend/server.py:866-985.

action_items: (Optional) Decide whether Ethical Penalty category should match for ALL profiles or only when profile_type=='ethical'. Current implementation routes McDonalds/KFC/Primark/H&M/Amazon/Shein/Pepsi/Coca-Cola/Nestlé/Monsanto to 'Ethical Penalty' for every user. The new test test_mcdonalds_routed_to_fast_food_under_balanced accepts either Ethical Penalty or Fast Food precisely to flag this ambiguity — please confirm intended behaviour.
Optional regression: add a UI Settings screen test once frontend integrates GET/PATCH /api/settings (not in scope here).
critical_code_review_comments: Categories are matched in a server-defined order (Ethical Penalty FIRST). This is profile-agnostic and may surprise users with non-ethical profiles. Consider gating with profile_type or moving routing to a per-profile keyword-overlay strategy.

apply_profile_multiplier() always caps with min(max_rate, rate). For Ethical Penalty (max 0.70) this is fine, but for Coffee max=0.50 the aggressive 0.30*1.5=0.45 and mindful 0.30*0.5=0.15 fit comfortably — verified.
Savings-Beast auto-transfer only fires inside /tax/process. If pending taxes were accumulated outside that endpoint (none currently) they wouldn't auto-transfer. Acceptable for current scope.

_revolut_callback_url derives redirect_uri from FastAPI request.base_url; in a Kubernetes ingress this matches the public URL because FastAPI sees the forwarded scheme/host. If proxy headers were missing, redirect_uri could land on an internal hostname — keep an eye on ingress config when promoting beyond preview.

passlib trips a benign WARNING reading bcrypt version on startup (not an error) — see /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log. Functional, but worth pinning bcrypt<4 or upgrading passlib in a future iteration.
seed_data_creation: Each test class registers fresh users (prefix 'test_i4_*@eva-test.com'); per-test users created on the fly. No persistent seed data added; no production data mutated.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 4 totals 50 backend pytest cases (in /app/backend/tests/). To run: `set -a && source /app/frontend/.env && set +a && pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v`. EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL is the public preview URL. Existing tests are independent of iteration_4 tests; user state isolation per-test means re-running is safe. Note the Ethical Penalty category-precedence quirk above — if main agent changes _match_category, re-run TestEthicalPenaltyPrecedence.
